*** 1 INJ *** STRAIGHT LINE WINDS WERE OBSERVED TO HAVE CAUSED SEVERAL DOWNED TREES AND MINOR OUTBUILDING DAMAGE NEAR DECATUR CREEK AND GOODFIELD ROAD. A TORNADO DEVELOPED JUST SOUTHWEST OF THE CORNER OF SHARP ROAD AND HIGHWAY 58 AS A CONVERGENT PATTERN IN THE DAMAGE PATH WAS OBSERVED. THIS TORNADO TRACKED NORTHEAST ACROSS HIGHWAY 58 AND ENDED JUST SOUTH OF CRESTVIEW LANE. ESTIMATED MAX WINDS OF 75 MPH WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 0.57 MILES AND PATH WIDTH OF 150 YARDS.

A SMALL TORNADO PATH WAS SURVEYED IN EASTERN LEE COUNTY, VIRGINIA, APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES WEST SOUTHWEST OF PENNINGTON GAP. THE TRACK WAS ABOVE LEFT POOR VALLEY ROAD BELOW THE RIDGELINE OF POOR VALLEY RIDGE. MAXIMUM WIND SPEED WAS 100 MPH. AN EF1 TORNADO. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 0.15 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS.

A SMALL TORNADO PATH WAS SURVEYED IN EASTERN LEE COUNTY, VIRGINIA, APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES WEST SOUTHWEST OF PENNINGTON GAP. THE TRACK WAS ABOVE LEFT POOR VALLEY ROAD BELOW THE RIDGELINE OF POOR VALLEY RIDGE. MAXIMUM WIND SPEED WAS 60 MPH. AN EF0 TORNADO. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 0.15 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS.
A NWS SURVEY TEAM INVESTIGATED DAMAGE IN PENNINGTON GAP, VIRGINIA. A 2.5 MILE WIDE SWATH OF DAMAGE WAS FOUND ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF TOWN. WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED BETWEEN 70 AND 90 MPH.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND A DAMAGE PATH THAT BEGAN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 58 AND THE WEST END OF OLD RIDGE ROAD, WHERE TREES WERE UPROOTED. THE PATH CROSSED A RIDGE, AND ENDED NEAR THE EAST END OF OLD RIDGE ROAD. MAXIMUM WIND SPEED WAS 100 MPH, AN EF1 TORNADO. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 1.2 MILES, PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS.

The tornado touched down in the community of Oral in eastern Roane County near the intersection of Country Lane and Highway 70. The tornado tracked eastward along Highway 70 before dissipating near Oral Cemetery. The most significant damage occurred near the beginning of the track with damage to detached garage structures with significant roof damage to homes. Max winds were estimated at 95 mph with a path length of 0.5 miles and a path width of 200 yards.
Trees down and roof damage to a few homes.

Loudon County High sustained significant roof damage to the gymnasium. The roof decking was ripped off the building. The only other structural damage on site was to the ticket booth, which also had the roof lifted off and destroyed. Wind speeds are estimated to be 75 mph.

Tree and roof damage in the neighborhoods north and south of I-40 along Lovell road.

Tornado reached peak intensity and width as it affected Lovell Cove subdivision and the Lovell Crossing apartment complex. Maximum width was observed to be 200 yards, with a maximum damage rating of EF-2 and 130 mph winds at the Lovell Crossing complex. Several buildings had substantial roof damage, with an onsite storage building completely collapsed as well. Additionally, significant roof damage was noted to homes in the Lovel Cove subdivision on Sugarfield Lane, adjacent on the west side of the apartment complex.
ASOS station KCHA Chattanooga Airport.

Straight line wind damage began just west of the Splendor Oaks subdivision off of Highway 139 in Sevier county. One large tree was snapped near the base of the trunk, with another uprooted. Additional large branches were snapped off other trees. Damage intensified further east along 139 as it moved into neighboring Jefferson county and affected the Overlook RV Park. Peak wind speeds are estimated at 85 mph.
